message:
type: CLAIM
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
summary: Adding opt-in debug trace logging to StreamListener (start/end/flush/reset/emit)

type: CLAIM
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
summary: Adding idle_timeout_s and tick() heartbeat/timeout mechanism to StreamListener

type: INTENT
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
summary: Will add __init__ param idle_timeout_s + self._last_chunk_ts, update timestamp in receive() on non-empty chunk, add new tick(now_ts=None) method, and extract the stream-end reset block (stream_end/cache_hit/field_start_queue/field_end_queue/stream_start) into a small _reset_state() helper reused by receive() and tick().
blocked_on: please confirm if your trace logging also touches the reset block in receive() or adds new methods, so we don't collide

type: STATUS
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
summary: Done: added idle_timeout_s param, _last_chunk_ts tracking in receive(), and tick() method emitting empty-chunk StreamResponse + unconditional state reset on timeout

type: ANSWER
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
summary: My debug logging adds one guarded self._logger.debug(...) call inside the existing reset block in receive() (stream_end/cache_hit/field_start_queue/field_end_queue/stream_start), plus similar guarded log calls at start-detection, rolling-end-check, emit, and flush() sites. I do not rename/extract that block into a helper, so your _reset_state() extraction should be a mechanical refactor around my added log line -- just keep the debug log call in the extracted helper so both reset paths (receive() and tick()) emit it.

type: STATUS
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
summary: Done: added debug/debug_logger params, guarded DEBUG-level trace logs (start detection x2, rolling end check, emit chunk, flush, state reset) with 80-char truncated buffer previews. All existing streaming tests pass.
